{"id":"project-LICHEN-ccjp","title":"Router and ForwardingBuffer lack thread safety","description":"File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py (multiple methods)

Neither Router nor ForwardingBuffer have any locking or synchronization primitives, but they manage mutable state that could be accessed from multiple contexts:

1. ForwardingBuffer.try_buffer() modifies _buffer and _source_order (lines 191-225)
2. ForwardingBuffer.expire_old() iterates and modifies _buffer (lines 268-283)
3. Router.expire_pending() iterates and modifies pending_queue (lines 577-588)
4. Router.dtn_buffer_message/dtn_retrieve_for modify dtn_buffer (lines 645-710)

WHY IT'S A BUG: If used in an async or threaded context (common for network routers), concurrent calls could cause:
- Race in try_buffer: two threads see same count, both add, exceed max
- Race in expire_old: thread A iterating, thread B dequeuing same entry
- Race in dtn methods: _dtn_buffer_bytes counter could become inconsistent

IMPACT: Data corruption, incorrect buffer accounting, duplicate/lost packets in concurrent scenarios.

FIX: Either document as 'single-threaded only' with prominent warnings, or add locking. Consider threading.Lock for critical sections or queue.Queue for producer/consumer patterns.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:34.435053Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:34.435095Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-ccjp","title":"Router and ForwardingBuffer lack thread safety","description":"File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py (multiple methods)

Neither Router nor ForwardingBuffer have any locking or synchronization primitives, but they manage mutable state that could be accessed from multiple contexts:

1. ForwardingBuffer.try_buffer() modifies _buffer and _source_order (lines 191-225)
2. ForwardingBuffer.expire_old() iterates and modifies _buffer (lines 268-283)
3. Router.expire_pending() iterates and modifies pending_queue (lines 577-588)
4. Router.dtn_buffer_message/dtn_retrieve_for modify dtn_buffer (lines 645-710)

WHY IT'S A BUG: If used in an async or threaded context (common for network routers), concurrent calls could cause:
- Race in try_buffer: two threads see same count, both add, exceed max
- Race in expire_old: thread A iterating, thread B dequeuing same entry
- Race in dtn methods: _dtn_buffer_bytes counter could become inconsistent

IMPACT: Data corruption, incorrect buffer accounting, duplicate/lost packets in concurrent scenarios.

FIX: Either document as 'single-threaded only' with prominent warnings, or add locking. Consider threading.Lock for critical sections or queue.Queue for producer/consumer patterns.","notes":"Commit: 3a3c35963b112c1a0a20be7f8bea1bf672f61d29","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:34.435053Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T19:49:34.067883076Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Merged relevant fixes from worker11 commit 3a5390de7ab7d6448e2a43a9717e8d5f21b85119 (close method race fix in sim_client, router.py updates). 1/3 codereview pass complete: no P0-P2. Thread safety still open. New hash after local edit: pending-commit."}
